src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Introduce a new ROLE_USER entity to support a multi-user architecture with distinct permissions.
Define OpenAPI security schemes for Basic Auth, Bearer JWT, and Session Cookies to document authentication methods.
Update API version and contact information to reflect the new release and support details.
Inject UserDataInitializationService to handle per-user data initialization in the new multi-user architecture.
Update existing users to have all roles (USER, ADMIN, AGENT) to ensure compatibility with the new multi-role system.
Refactor user creation logic to assign appropriate roles based on user type (USER vs ADMIN/AGENT).
Initialize sample data per admin user instead of globally, supporting the multi-user architecture.
Integrated a custom JWT authentication filter into the security chain to support stateless API authentication.
Explicitly configured session management policy to allow sessions if required, supporting hybrid web/API usage.
Customized the HTTP Basic authentication entry point to return JSON errors for API requests instead of redirecting to a login page.
Added new public endpoints for registration, Swagger UI, and OAuth/JWT auth flows to the permitAll list.
Refined role-based access control to include AGENT and USER roles for specific API and web endpoints.
Restrict access to the new flight page to users with the ADMIN role.
Restrict access to the save flight endpoint to users with the ADMIN role.
Restrict access to the delete flight endpoint to users with the ADMIN role.
Update flight retrieval logic to filter results based on the current user's permissions or context.
Add @PreAuthorize annotation to restrict airport management endpoints to ADMIN role.
Add @PreAuthorize annotation to restrict aircraft management endpoints to ADMIN role.
Remove the custom login page endpoint, likely delegating to Spring Security's default login mechanism.
Add a ManyToOne relationship to a User entity to support multi-user architecture where aircraft have an owner.
Add a ManyToOne relationship to a User entity to support multi-user architecture where airports have an owner.
Add an owner field to the Flight model to support multi-user architecture by linking flights to a specific user.
Add a ManyToOne relationship to a User entity to support multiuser architecture by tracking passenger ownership.
Add an 'enabled' boolean field to the User model to support account activation status.
Add an 'accountNonExpired' boolean field to the User model to support account expiration status.
Add an 'accountNonLocked' boolean field to the User model to support account lock status.
Add a 'credentialsNonExpired' boolean field to the User model to support credential expiration status.
Add a 'currentRole' string field to the User model to store the active role of the user.
